construct.label: Economic and vocational anxiety
construct.definition: Anxiety arising from the belief that advancing AI systems may reduce the future value, bargaining power, status, or transferability of occupational skills.
construct.subconstruct: occupational status and prestige loss in creative work

scenario_text: Imagine that advertising agencies, publishers, and entertainment studios begin using AI systems to generate polished concept art, scripts, logos, storyboards, music drafts, and marketing images in minutes. Human creative professionals are still hired for direction, taste, client negotiation, and final selection, but public discussion increasingly treats much of the craft as inexpensive and easily automated. Clients begin questioning premium fees for work they believe AI can produce quickly.

response_scale.prompt: How anxious would this scenario make you about AI reducing the occupational status or prestige of creative professionals?

scoring.missing_response_rule: Treat skipped responses as missing at the item level. Do not impute single-item scores for primary analyses.
exclusion_criteria: Exclude respondents who fail the survey-level attention check specified in the study protocol.
Exclude respondents who complete the item in less than one-third of the median reading time for the administration wave.
Exclude respondents who report that they could not understand the scenario after reading it.
Exclude respondents younger than the minimum eligible age for the study population.

interpretation_bands.label: low
interpretation_bands.interpretation: Respondent reports little or no anxiety about AI reducing the occupational status or prestige of creative professionals.

interpretation_bands.label: moderate
interpretation_bands.interpretation: Respondent reports a meaningful but not severe anxiety response to creative-work status loss under AI adoption.

interpretation_bands.label: high
interpretation_bands.interpretation: Respondent reports strong anxiety that AI could reduce the prestige, perceived craft value, or occupational standing of creative work.

population.target_population: Adults in the general population, with planned subgroup analysis for writers, artists, designers, musicians, media workers, and creative freelancers.
population.eligible_respondents: Respondents aged 18 or older who can complete the survey language without assistance and provide informed consent.
exclusion_criteria: Current inability to provide informed consent.
Duplicate panel participation within the same measurement wave.
Self-reported inability to read the survey language.

administration_mode.instructions: Present the scenario text exactly as written, followed immediately by the response prompt and the five ordered response options.
administration_mode.randomization: Randomize item order within the economic_vocational domain block while preserving the ordered response scale.
administration_mode.longitudinal_use: Use identical wording, response anchors, scoring, and eligibility rules across waves unless a new item version is released.

ethics_flags.data_protection_notes: Do not collect employer names, client names, portfolio identifiers, income records, or identifiable intellectual-property claims for this item.
ethics_flags.irb_notes: The item presents a plausible status-loss scenario and may induce mild concern about professional identity or prestige. Include a debrief clarifying that the scenario is hypothetical and not a prediction about any respondent's creative field.
